title: Microsoft puts Hugging Face models on Foundry Managed Compute
friendlyTitle: Microsoft is making Hugging Face models easier for companies to run
summary: Microsoft and Hugging Face detailed a Foundry integration that lets enterprises deploy curated open-weight Hugging Face models onto Foundry Managed Compute. The setup promises pre-staged weights in Azure, scanned runtimes, unified billing, observability, governance, and support for runtimes such as vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, NIM, TEI, and llama.cpp.
friendlySummary: Hugging Face models can now be deployed through Microsoft Foundry Managed Compute with enterprise security, monitoring, and billing tools.
whyItMatters: Open models are becoming easier to run inside enterprise platforms instead of bespoke infrastructure projects. That gives teams more room to mix frontier APIs, open weights, private networking, residency controls, and cost planning under one operating model.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Companies may get more choices than only using big closed AI APIs. They can run open models with more control over cost, data, and deployment.
